stem: A release enters the software release pipeline. The probability of passing code review is 94%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing unit testing is between 66% and 77% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing integration testing is 79%. What is the overall probability that the release is deployed without incidents?

ground_truth.answer: P(deployed without incidents) ranges from 0.49 to 0.572 as the unit testing pass rate varies from 66% to 77%. Point estimate: 0.535.

solution_steps: The release must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the unit testing stage creates a 8.2 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the unit testing rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
